rows.description: Soluta Varia.
rows.notes: Page title clearly written at top centre; no amounts on the same line.

rows.description: Acct Dinner
rows.notes: Clear label introducing a braced group of dinner sub-items; no total printed on same line with the label itself (sub-items follow).
Handwritten as 'wine - 0.11.8' within the brace. Column alignment consistent with other subitems; pence 8 is clear.

rows.description: wood - 0.3.0
rows.notes: Clear handwriting 'wood - 0.3.0'. Numbers align with pounds/shillings/pence pattern and are unambiguous.

rows.description: Coquo - 1.18.4
rows.notes: Item reads 'Coquo - 1.18.4' (possibly 'Cooke' or surname). Numerals are clear; shillings 18 and pence 4 valid.

rows.description: Coffe - 0.1.0
rows.notes: Clear 'Coffe - 0.1.0' (coffee). Numbers legible and align with columns.
Total for the 'Acct Dinner' group appears in rightmost columns as '2 14 0'. Alignment of totals row is unambiguous.

rows.description: To Mr Gaymer teacher of Hebr.
rows.notes: Description legible as 'To Mr Gaymer teacher of ...'; amount '2 0 0' clearly written in columns. Surname reading 'Gaymer' certain; final word slightly abbreviated but not needed for amount.

rows.description: Tax for Thrupp
rows.notes: 'Tax for Thrupp' clear; amount '0 15 0' aligned and legible. Shillings 15 within valid range.

rows.description: To Pater for Founders of Coll. pictures
rows.notes: Reads 'To Pater for Founders of Coll. pictures' (some abbreviations). Amount '1 0 0' clear in the right columns.

rows.description: To Mr Adams for ye Progresse
rows.notes: Handwriting 'To Mr Adams for ye Progresse' readable; amount '5 18 0' clear. Slight uncertainty on final word spelling, not on numerals.

rows.description: wood for ye Burying
rows.notes: Description 'wood for ye Burying' legible; amount '0 1 3' clearly aligned. Pence 3 unambiguous.

rows.description: A bell rope for ye chapell
rows.notes: Phrase 'A bell rope for ye chapell' is clear; amount '0 4 0' readable in columns.

rows.description: Buttery book nat 3.2.8 St John's Bapt. 12.0
rows.notes: Description is somewhat cramped and abbreviated; appears to list internal itemised amounts '3.2.8' and '12.0' followed by a total. Right-side columns read '2 4 6'. Chosen '2 4 6' because digits align there; description transcription uncertain due to abbreviations and cramped script.

rows.description: To Mr Pitfield due to hir Husband for work
rows.notes: Reading 'To Mr Pitfield due to her Husband for work' (her spelled 'hir'); amount '17 14 10' clearly visible though pence 10 is near column boundary—alignment consistent.

rows.description: To Mr Collison for work &c.
rows.notes: Large amount '45 7 9' clearly written and aligned with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling. Name 'Collison' readable.

rows.description: To James Davy in pt. 6 more 12
rows.notes: Description 'To James Davy in pt. 6 more 12' somewhat abbreviated but legible; amount '22 0 0' clear in right columns.

rows.description: To Mr Wildgoose for work
rows.notes: 'To Mr Wildgoose for work' readable; numeric '1 2 8' clear and within valid ranges.

rows.description: To John Coe for 11 yd 8½ of matting for ye Chapel
rows.notes: Text 'To John Coe for 11yd 8½ of matting for ye Chapel' mostly legible; amount '0 8 10' clear. The half-yard fraction is in the description, not the amount.

rows.description: To Stubbs ye Gardiner
rows.notes: Name reads 'Stubbs' (or 'Stubby') and 'Gardiner' clear; amount '0 12 10' legible in columns.

rows.description: To Mr Wyse for 18 yd of ??? cloth for ye Fellows' table & making
rows.notes: Description partly obscured/abbreviated; likely 'Mr Wyse' or similar. Amount '1 0 0' clearly visible, but the exact spelling of the name and some words are uncertain.

rows.description: To Wain ye Farrier by consent 3 more 18s
rows.notes: Handwriting here is faint and compressed; right columns read approximately '1 4 6' though there is slight bleed from adjacent ink. Description inferred as 'Wain ye Farrier' but could be different; chosen amounts match visible digits but with moderate uncertainty.

rows.description: To Cognis for xtmas now yrs & 12 day
rows.notes: Description abbreviations ('Cognis' possibly 'Congr?s' or 'Coignet'); numeric '10 10 6' is clear and well aligned. Context suggests seasonal payment.

rows.description: To Mr Gainger for last year
rows.notes: Clear description and amount '1 0 0' visible in columns.

rows.description: Candles & W: Coffins Ladyday g:8:8 Mich:6:3:8
rows.notes: Phrase is abbreviated: appears to list candle/coffin charges at Ladyday and Michaelmas; combined amount in right columns reads '15 12 0'. Some abbreviation ambiguous but numerals are legible.

rows.description: Mr Broadwater
rows.notes: Single name 'Mr Broadwater' (or Broadbroke) and amount '13 8 0' clear in columns. Name reading somewhat dependent on flourished letters but largely legible.

rows.description: To Clark ye Smith
rows.notes: Clear 'To Clark ye Smith' and numeric '9 7 7' legible. Alignment with ruling lines strong.

rows.description: To Mr Bugge by consent
rows.notes: Name 'Bugge' readable; amount '1 1 6' clear though script slightly narrow.

rows.description: To Hilliard for Periams building Lead
rows.notes: Surnamed 'Hilliard' is clear; 'Periams' (building name) uncertain but amount '24 4 3' is legible. Some letters in the description are abbreviated making the exact building name unclear.

rows.description: To Mr Townsend remainder in full for Periams building
rows.notes: Description uses abbreviation 'remainder in full'; amount '7 11 10' readable. 'Periams' remains somewhat uncertain but consistent with previous line.

rows.description: To Mr Hilliard two bills for glasing Periams building & preparations
rows.notes: Entry references Hilliard again; 'glasing' (glazing) is legible. Numeric '6 13 0' aligns cleanly though some ink blotting nearby reduces certainty.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Hilliard for glazing at Periam's building and associated preparatory work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Hilliard for glazing at Periam's building and associated preparatory work, amounting to £6 13s 0d.

rows.description: To Watts & Browne viewers for three Ponne Leases
rows.notes: Names and last words are faint and abbreviated ('Ponne' uncertain). Amount '2 7 10' clear and within range; description text uncertain in a few letters.
rows.english_description: Payment to Watts & Browne for acting as viewers (surveyors) in relation to three Ponne leases.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Watts & Browne for acting as viewers (surveyors) in relation to three Ponne leases, amounting to £2 7s 10d.

rows.description: To Mr Clarke for pwn wk & paper &c
rows.notes: Description heavily abbreviated and partially overwritten; right-side numerals appear to read '12 14 9' though there is extra ink/erasure nearby. Chose these digits based on alignment with the three vertical ruling lines; moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Clarke for pen work, paper and other sundry office/stationery items.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Clarke for pen work, paper and other sundry office/stationery items, amounting to £12 14s 9d.

rows.description: To Mr Wallisfare for nails, for Carpenters, Joyners & Plasters
rows.notes: Name possibly 'Wallisfare' or 'Wallispare'; trades and purpose are readable ('nails...Carpenters...Plasters'). Amount '1 6 8' visible but small and slightly smudged.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Wallisfare for nails supplied for carpenters, joiners and plasterers.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Wallisfare for nails supplied for carpenters, joiners and plasterers, amounting to £1 6s 8d.

rows.description: To Mr Acland by consent for Ann Shipond
rows.notes: Surname and beneficiary ('Ann Shipond') faint and abbreviated. Numeric '0 1 6' is visible but vertical alignment close to adjacent lines, so moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Acland by consent on behalf of Ann Shipond (beneficiary).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Acland by consent on behalf of Ann Shipond, amounting to £0 1s 6d.

rows.description: For cleansing ye yard behind ye Lodgings
rows.notes: Description readable but cramped; amount looks like '0 1 6' (same as previous row) though ink thinning makes the exact digits less certain. Possibly a small cleaning fee.
rows.english_description: Payment for cleansing the yard behind the lodgings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for cleansing the yard behind the lodgings, amounting to £0 1s 6d.

rows.description: By city of Oxford fines for 3 cases viz: Wilmot, Lamb & Waller
rows.notes: Clear phrase 'city of Oxford fines for 3 cases' and list of names; amount '12 0 0' distinct and well-formed.
rows.english_description: Payment of fines imposed by the City of Oxford in three cases involving Wilmot, Lamb and Waller.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of fines imposed by the City of Oxford in three cases involving Wilmot, Lamb and Waller, amounting to £12 0s 0d.

rows.description: Fees & other charges for ye same at 2: 8: 4 &c
rows.notes: Description shows '2:8:4' as component; total amount column reads '8 9 0' though the connection between shown intermediate numbers and the total is not explicit. The numerals are legible but some ink marks nearby reduce clarity.
rows.english_description: Additional fees and other charges relating to the same three town cases.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Additional fees and other charges relating to the same three town cases, amounting to £8 9s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Fowell for soliciting three Town Cases
rows.notes: This row is problematic: the pence column appears to read '12' which is not a valid pence (should be 0–11). That indicates likely misalignment or smudge. The intended value might be '6 12 0' or '6 12 0' or '1 6 0'. Because the pence digit is ambiguous and misaligned, confidence is low. I recorded '1 6 12' strictly following faint marks but note that pence 12 violates validation and the actual reading is uncertain.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Fowell for soliciting and representing the college in three town cases.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Fowell for soliciting and representing the college in three town cases, amounting to £1 6s 12d.

rows.description: For a yearly tax to Queen Ann due at Ladyday 1713
rows.notes: Text 'yearly tax to Queen Ann' clear; numeric group looks like '6 12 0' but digits are slightly faint and cramped. Chosen values consistent with visible strokes but moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Payment of the annual tax due to Queen Anne at Lady Day 1713.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of the annual tax due to Queen Anne at Lady Day 1713, amounting to £6 12s 0d.

rows.description: To Coquet for pewell on part 20 more in full 2:0
rows.notes: Lower portion of page has heavy ink fade and cramped script. The leftmost pounds figure reads as '46' (large, slightly smudged). The description contains multiple abbreviations; chosen amount '46 0 0' follows the most legible digits but uncertainty about exact pounds remains moderate-high.
rows.english_description: Payment to Coquet in full settlement relating to a pew/’pewell’ on part twenty (balance paid).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Coquet in full settlement relating to a pew/’pewell’ on part twenty, amounting to £46 0s 0d.

rows.description: Sum
rows.notes: Bottom-right shows a final 'Sum' and figures read as '287 7 8d' (pence 8 clear). Some surrounding ink smudging but overall total is readable. Confidence moderate because some earlier rows had uncertainties that feed into the total.
